stdout:
## Success criteria from issue description - **Montgomery internals + API preservation:** Met (`crates/gf2-core/src/gfp/mod.rs:35,105-113,126-131,173-190`; `crates/gf2-core/src/gfp/montgomery.rs:74-103`). - **Constant-time hot path (add/sub/mul):** Met in implementation style; `mont_add`, `mont_sub`, and `redc` are branchless w.r.t. element data (`montgomery.rs:74-83,111-127`), and `mul` dispatch is only on compile-time modulus shape (`mod.rs:184-189`). - **Cross-verification requirement:** **Not fully met as written**; `Fp<65537>` and `Fp<2^61-1>` provide 10k random ops each via proptest (`mod.rs:694-761`), but `Fp<3>` uses exhaustive checks instead of a 10k random harness (`mod.rs:629-639,688-693`). - **Benchmark threshold evidence:** Partially addressed via `crates/gf2-core/benches/BENCHMARK_RESULTS.md:1-30` and naive baselines with `black_box(p)` in bench code (`benches/fp_montgomery.rs:151-186`), but evidence is a static markdown artifact (measured on older commit per line 3) rather than enforced/verified at gate time. ## Separation of concerns - Pass: changes are in `gf2-core` surfaces (`crates/gf2-core/src/gfp/*`, `crates/gf2-core/benches/*`), and `gf2-core` has no dependency on `gf2-coding` (`crates/gf2-core/Cargo.toml:12-19`). ## Functional paradigm and performance - Pass with caveat: high-level API remains trait-driven and clean (`mod.rs`), low-level arithmetic is efficient word-level code (`montgomery.rs`), and GF(p) ops are implemented with bitwise/wrapping arithmetic where appropriate. - Caveat: performance acceptance relies on manually committed benchmark results rather than reproducible pass/fail checks. ## Correctness - Pass for reviewed arithmetic: const computations and REDC are well-covered (`montgomery.rs:163-425`), and cross-checks against naive modular formulas exist across small and large primes (`mod.rs:629-761`). - `BitVec` tail masking / bit numbering invariants are unaffected by this change set (no touched `bitvec` mutation paths). ## Testing - Available gate artifacts show green fmt/clippy/tests for this issue’s latest validated code-review baseline commit (`.jit/gate-runs/700bc089...`, `9151b333...`, `51e46ef0...` all `status: passed`). - Fail on issue acceptance strictness: required cross-verification wording (“10,000+ random operations across Fp<3>, Fp<65537>, Fp<2^61-1>”) is not satisfied literally for `Fp<3>`. ## Unsafe isolation - Pass: no new `unsafe` in touched `gf2-core` files; unsafe isolation policy remains intact. ## Documentation - Public `Fp

` docs are present and updated for Montgomery representation (`mod.rs:1-71,90-132`). - Benchmark evidence documentation was added (`benches/BENCHMARK_RESULTS.md`), but it introduces review debt because acceptance is documented, not machine-validated. ## Prior review feedback for this issue - **Addressed:** missing benchmark evidence now has an explicit results document (`BENCHMARK_RESULTS.md`). - **Still unresolved:** latest prior feedback’s core point about explicit 10k random coverage including `Fp<3>` remains unmet in literal form (still exhaustive there, not random). ## Jit issue dependencies - Pass: prerequisite issue `350bff7f` is marked done and this work builds on that baseline. VERDICT: FAIL --- Gate result: FAILED
